content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the diff and read the prompt-generation and prompt-schema changes end-to-end. Targeted existing tests pass, but I found 2 correctness issues in planned-elsewhere rendering: reordered implicit plans can make the prompt list the active bead as work planned elsewhere, and fence-first related-bead descriptions can break prompt generation entirely.

## Amendments

1. Fix the `planned_elsewhere` location hint so it cannot use an unconfirmed title-fallback slot as if it were authoritative. In `src/cli/project.rs:884-889`, `resolve_bead_plan` records `matched_workstream_index`/`matched_bead_index` even when `membership_confirmed` is `false`. `build_planned_elsewhere_context` then prefers that unconfirmed location at `src/cli/project.rs:1121-1124`, and the neighbor scan only excludes the matched index, not the live bead id (`src/cli/project.rs:1140-1164`). With a reordered implicit plan whose old title still matches, this can add the current bead itself to `## Already Planned Elsewhere` (for example, `ms-alpha.bead-2 ... adjacent same-workstream bead`). Use only confirmed or implicit-slot-derived locations for adjacency, and/or skip any candidate whose `proposal_id == bead.id`.
   Rationale: This produces a self-contradictory prompt that tells the agent the active bead is already planned elsewhere, which can mis-scope or suppress the very work the project was created to execute.
2. Make `planned_elsewhere` summary compaction fence-safe. `compact_planned_elsewhere_summary` in `src/cli/project.rs:958-964` keeps only the first non-empty line of a related bead description. If that first line is a fence opener like `````md`, `format_planned_elsewhere` injects the dangling opener verbatim into the generated prompt (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:885-902`). Because `create_project_from_bead_context` immediately revalidates the canonical prompt shape (`src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:407-417`), the unclosed fence causes later `## Review Policy` / `## AGENTS / Repo Guidance` headings to disappear from validation and `project create-from-bead` fails. Strip or close fences during compaction, or choose a non-fence textual summary line instead of blindly taking the first non-empty line.
   Rationale: A valid adjacent bead description can currently make prompt generation fail outright, so the new prompt builder is not robust against normal markdown content in milestone plan descriptions.
